title: Aureline browser-handoff packet and provider-actor vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the typed browser-handoff packet and supporting connected-provider vocabulary frozen in docs/adr/0010-connected-provider-browser-handoff-approval-ticket.md. Every launch of the system browser from a protected Aureline surface reads a browser_handoff_packet_record rather than a raw URL. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, and raw delegated-token bytes never cross the RPC boundary; packets carry opaque destination_ref and intent_signature fields instead. Three record kinds are exported: browser_handoff_packet_record (the minted handoff), browser_handoff_audit_event_record (an observable event on the provider_handoff audit stream), and connected_provider_record (one registry row describing who Aureline is acting as on the provider). The eventual connected-provider crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new destination_class, provider_actor_class, reason_code, replay_posture, grant_resolution_reason, health_state, redaction_class, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and requires a provider_handoff_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provider_handoff_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this provider-handoff vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary. 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class; it exists so a surface that cannot resolve the actor routes to a repair hook rather than falling back to a generic 'Connected' badge. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, support-export, review-overlay, and audit surface.

destination_class.description: Frozen destination-class vocabulary. The system-browser launcher resolves destination_ref to a concrete URL at launch time; raw URLs are never passed through by arbitrary callers.

reason_code.description: Frozen reason-for-handoff vocabulary. Every packet names exactly one reason. A packet whose reason_code corresponds to a mutation-class action MUST reference the approval ticket that admitted it or carry reason_code = provider_consent_flow.

replay_posture.description: Frozen replay-posture vocabulary. 'single_use' is the default; 'bounded_reuse' requires an explicit counter and bounded window; 'read_only_resumable' is allowed only for anchors whose associated action_class is read_only_local_analysis or whose mutation_mode is inspect_only.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class used by logs_local, traces_local, support_bundle, evidence_packet, ai_context_capture, recipe_manifest, profile_export, sync, crash_dump, mutation_journal_entry, save_manifest, replay_or_timeline_capture, terminal_transcript, and clipboard_projection surfaces. See ADR-0007 redaction posture and ADR-0010 redaction defaults.

grant_resolution_reason.description: Frozen grant-resolution reason codes shared across provider badges, publish-later queue items, support exports, and audit streams.

provider_class.description: Frozen provider-class vocabulary. Gives one stable anchor for UX, CLI inspection, support export, and policy evaluation.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the provider_handoff stream. Events carry actor subject, actor class, connected-provider id, packet / ticket ids, mutation mode, grant-resolution reason, policy epoch, and trust state where relevant. Events MUST NOT carry raw tokens, raw URLs, or raw callback bodies.

host_identity.description: Canonical host identity that minted the packet (shell / supervisor process identity).

workspace_id.description: Workspace id the packet was minted from (ADR-0006 / ADR-0009).

actor_subject.description: Stable subject reference for the acting identity; the subject shape corresponds to the packet's expected_authority_on_destination actor class.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id the packet was minted inside.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch at mint time (ADR-0008 / ADR-0009). A callback presented at a later policy epoch is re-evaluated.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical origin an inbound callback validates against before mutating local state. An inbound callback whose correlator does not match a live packet, or whose origin_disclosure does not verify, is denied with browser_handoff_callback_rejected.

policy_context.description: Policy context the packet was minted under. Every spend re-validates this context.

bounded_reuse_counter.description: Bounded-reuse counter. Present only when replay_posture is bounded_reuse.

packet_schema_version.description: Integer schema version pinned into the packet so a replayed packet can be validated against the exact shape it was minted under.

destination_ref.description: Opaque destination token or canonical provider route. The system-browser launcher resolves this to a concrete URL at launch time; callers MUST NOT pass raw URLs through destination_ref.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the handoff targets.

disclosure_summary.description: Human-legible paragraph naming the destination class, the object identity, and the data-loss / privacy consequence of leaving Aureline scope. A packet without a disclosure_summary is refused at mint.

return_anchor.description: Stable anchor for returning the user to the originating Aureline object. Round-trips independently of whether the remote system preserves query strings.

intent_signature.description: Host-signed intent envelope binding packet_id, destination_class, destination_ref, object_identity, reason_code, expected_authority_on_destination, origin_disclosure, replay_posture, issued_at, and expires_at. The signature is what makes the packet replay-safe; a packet whose intent_signature cannot be verified is denied with origin_signature_invalid.

callback_correlator.description: Optional correlator for a provider callback that completes the handoff. Inbound callbacks are validated against the packet's origin_disclosure and replay_posture before mutating state.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque reference to the approval-ticket record that admitted this handoff. Required when the handoff corresponds to a mutation-class action; null only when reason_code is provider_consent_flow.

connected_provider_record_id.description: Opaque reference to the connected_provider_record whose actor class the handoff is acting under.

revoked_at.description: Populated when the packet is revoked before launch or before callback. A revoked packet fires browser_handoff_revoked and fails any subsequent callback closed.

allOf.description: bounded_reuse_counter MUST be present when replay_posture is bounded_reuse, and MUST be absent otherwise.
read_only_resumable packets are read-only and MUST NOT carry an approval_ticket_ref.
A provider_consent_flow packet MAY omit approval_ticket_ref; every other mutation-class reason MUST carry one.

denial_reason.description: Optional typed denial reason on provider_action_denied / browser_handoff_callback_rejected / grant_revoked events. Silent downgrade is forbidden.

browser_handoff_audit_event_record.description: An observable event on the provider_handoff audit stream. Raw tokens, raw URLs, and raw callback bodies are forbidden on every audit-event record.

canonical_host.description: Canonical host the provider is bound to. Self-hosted and mirrored deployments substitute this host without changing the local object vocabulary.

tenant_or_org_scope.description: Tenant / org / project scope the provider record is bound under. Empty string only when the provider class is genuinely tenantless.

actor_classes_present.description: The set of actor classes this connected provider currently holds links for. A single generic 'Connected' state is forbidden; every provider renders each actor class distinctly.

linked_account_refs.description: Opaque references to human_account_link_records. Raw provider-side principal refs are held by the linked record, not here.

linked_delegated_credential_refs.description: Opaque references to delegated_credential_binding_records. Credential bodies live behind the ADR-0007 broker; this field carries ids only.

policy_owner.description: Opaque reference to the policy-bundle row that owns this provider record. Empty string when no admin policy is active.

connected_provider_record.description: Diagnostic snapshot of one connected provider. Support bundles MAY include provider_class, canonical_host, actor_classes_present, health_state, and last_grant_resolution_reason; they MUST NOT include raw tokens, raw install secrets, raw delegated-token bodies, or raw policy-injector material.
